title: Whole Mouse Brain Gelation and Digestion
author: Rajvi Javeri

steps.title: Prepare a new tube or vial...
steps.instruction: Prepare a new tube or vial with 3 mL MBS for each brain. Place On ice .

steps.title: Mix the dissolved AcX with MBS...
steps.instruction: Mix the dissolved AcX with MBS ( 2500 µg per brain or 250 µL of 10 mg/mL AcX with 3 mL MBS) for each whole brain and fill the remaining space with more MBS to minimize air.

steps.title: Incubate On ice at 4 °C...
steps.instruction: Incubate On ice at 4 °C , mix by inverting tubes once per day for 4 days.

steps.title: Replace solution with cold 1X PBS,...
steps.instruction: Replace solution with cold 1X PBS, mix by inverting, keep on On ice at 4 °C , Overnight .

steps.title: Replace solution with cold 1X PBS,...
steps.instruction: Replace solution with cold 1X PBS, mix by inverting, keep on On ice at 4 °C , Overnight .

steps.title: Prepare Stock X on On ice
steps.instruction: Prepare Stock X on On ice . Acrylamide powders and solutions are toxic if swallowed, inhaled, or absorbed through the skin. It is a mutagen, teratogen and a carcinogen. Dispose of acrylamide and any contaminated consumables in a hazardous waste stream.

steps.title: To activate Stock X, add VA-044
steps.instruction: To activate Stock X, add VA-044. The amount of VA-044 required is equal to 1.2% of the total volume of Stock X used. (Typically we add 234 µL of 10% (w/v) VA-044 in ~ 20 mL Stock X).

steps.title: Fill each whole brain tube with...
steps.instruction: Fill each whole brain tube with activated Stock X solution to minimize air.

steps.title: Save any extra activated Stock X solution on On ice
steps.instruction: Save any extra activated Stock X solution on On ice .

steps.title: Incubate on On ice at 4...
steps.instruction: Incubate on On ice at 4 °C , mix by inverting tubes once per day for 4 days.

steps.title: Bring brain vials and activated Stock...
steps.instruction: Bring brain vials and activated Stock X solution to Room temperature on the bench. Swirl Stock X conical tube carefully to avoid bubbles. Typically a 5 mL preparation of activated Stock X is more than sufficient for gelling one adult mouse brain. If more activated Stock X is required, a fresh preparation may be added to the Stock X left over from the previous step.

steps.title: De-gas activated Stock X solution in...
steps.instruction: De-gas activated Stock X solution in a vacuum chamber ~ 00:20:00 . This reduces the formation of bubbles during polymerization.

steps.title: Prepare polymerization chamber by stacking silicone...
steps.instruction: Prepare polymerization chamber by stacking silicone isolator gaskets of various thicknesses to reach a height of 6.5mm on an uncharged 1"x3" microscope slide. The gaskets should be stacked high enough so the entire mouse brain will be embedded within the hydrogel. Typically, we stack the gaskets to allow about 2 mm of gel to polymerize around the brain. Inspect chamber for dust and debris before beginning embedding process.

steps.title: Add activated Stock X to partly fill polymerization chamber
steps.instruction: Add activated Stock X to partly fill polymerization chamber.

steps.title: Avoid and eliminate all bubbles
steps.instruction: Avoid and eliminate all bubbles.

steps.title: Transfer brain to chamber and fill...
steps.instruction: Transfer brain to chamber and fill with Stock X just to the top.

steps.title: Seal chamber with a clean, long...
steps.instruction: Seal chamber with a clean, long coverslip, taking care to avoid bubbles.

steps.title: Place in petri dish, and seal...
steps.instruction: Place in petri dish, and seal in 2 sequential zip lock bags, each thoroughly purged with nitrogen gas.

steps.title: Incubate at 37 °C for 04:00:00, +
steps.instruction: Incubate at 37 °C for 04:00:00, + .

steps.title: Remove coverslip and gasket; gel should...
steps.instruction: Remove coverslip and gasket; gel should be firm without extra liquid dripping out.

steps.title: Cut gel with a razor blade,...
steps.instruction: Cut gel with a razor blade, making a rectangular cuboid shape. Leave about a 2 mm border of gel on all sides of the brain. If needed, the gel may be trimmed down further after expansion.

steps.title: Wash in 50 mL 1X PBS...
steps.instruction: Wash in 50 mL 1X PBS at Room temperature in a 50 mL conical tube for about 00:03:00 with swirling.

steps.title: Replace solution with 40 mL Proteinase...
steps.instruction: Replace solution with 40 mL Proteinase K (ProK) buffer spiked with 100U ProK (~ 126 µL ); incubate at Room temperature with gentle swirling or rocking on a nutator for 3 days. Store Proteinase K at -20 °C and keep On ice until added to the ProK Buffer.

steps.title: Swirl whole brain conical tube carefully and thoroughly
steps.instruction: Swirl whole brain conical tube carefully and thoroughly.

steps.title: Add 126 µL (100U) Proteinase K to the tube
steps.instruction: Add 126 µL (100U) Proteinase K to the tube.

steps.title: Move to 37 °C and incubate 5 days+
steps.instruction: Move to 37 °C and incubate 5 days+. Cortex should look transparent and the white matter should look mostly clear. Digestion time may be extended if needed.

steps.title: Wash brain in ~ 50 mL...
steps.instruction: Wash brain in ~ 50 mL 1X PBS briefly at Room temperature , then replace with enough 1X PBS to fill the 50 mL conical tube; wash at Room temperature with gentle swirling or rocking for 2 days.

steps.title: Replace 1X PBS solution for a few hours and/or Overnight
steps.instruction: Replace 1X PBS solution for a few hours and/or Overnight .

steps.title: Change final wash with 1X PBS Azide 0
steps.instruction: Change final wash with 1X PBS Azide 0.05% and store at 4 °C until ready for expansion.

steps.title: Dissolve 5 mg AcX in 500...
steps.instruction: Dissolve 5 mg AcX in 500 µL of anhydrous DMSO to create a 10 mg/mL solution and vortex to combine.

steps.title: Replace MBS again and store On...
steps.instruction: Replace MBS again and store On ice at 4 °C Overnight .

steps.title: Transfer brain samples to the 4 mL glass vials
steps.instruction: Transfer brain samples to the 4 mL glass vials.

steps.title: Wash sample in MBS at Room temperature
steps.instruction: Wash sample in MBS at Room temperature . Using a transfer pipette, fill vial to the top (typically 4 mL ). This will minimize the amount of air bubbles in the vial. Replace solution for each step: MBS for 01:00:00 MBS for 01:00:00
